channel.title: Ad News Feed
channel.description: 広告プラットフォーム最新情報の自動収集フィード

item.title: [Google Ads] SEO vs PPC: The Ultimate Marketing Strategy Revealed #shorts
item.description: The age-old marketing question: Should you focus on SEO or Google Ads first? The strategy has evolved. Build your SEO foundation, gain some traction, then scale with paid advertising. #SEO #GoogleAds #MarketingStrategy #PPC

item.title: [Google Ads] Ad Copy, Split Testing & AI for Google Ads
item.description: Stop Being Lazy With Your Google Ads Copy! Most advertisers are leaving money on the table because they rely on Google’s default recommendations. In this video, I’m showing you how to break away from the "one ad per ad group" trap and use Split Testing + AI to skyrocket your CTR and conversion rates

item.title: [Google Ads] Become a Google Partner: Requirements & Certifications Revealed! #shorts
item.description: Unlock Google Partner status! Focus on key certifications (Search, Video, Measurement & more) and meet spend requirements. Elevate your Google Ads expertise. #GooglePartner #GoogleAds #DigitalMarketing #SEM #PPC

item.title: [Meta Ads] Your Excuses Are Holding Back Your Results
item.description: Advertisers love to blame Meta when results tank, pointing to outages, Advantage+ enhancements, and automated settings as proof the platform is out to get them. There's a little truth in every complaint, which is exactly why they spread, but they're also excuses that keep you from finding actual sol

item.title: [Meta Ads] Meta and CBRE Invest in American Jobs Through New Fiber Technician Training Program
[General] Structured Data for LLMs: Technical Implementation Guide for Agency Dev Teams Using JSON-LD, Schema.org, and Entity Graphs
item.description: Large language models do not discover, parse, and trust web content the same way a human reader does. A person can infer that a page is about a service, that a named individual is the author, that a price belongs to a specific product, or that a business operates in a certain market even when [&#

item.title: [General] How Agencies Can Productize First-Party Data Collection for Recurring Revenue: A Practical Model for Pricing, Packaging, and Client Retention
item.description: Third-party signal loss did not remove the need for targeting, measurement, and personalization. It changed where the value sits. The value now sits closer to the customer relationship itself: consented website behavior, CRM records, lead form activity, purchase history, product interest signals,

item.title: [General] How to White-Label Autonomous Campaign Management Without Losing the Human Edge: An Agency Guide to Scale, Control, and Client Retention
item.description: White-label autonomous campaign management sounds simple until you try to operationalize it. On paper, the pitch is attractive. You give clients a branded service that uses automation, machine learning, rules, and AI-driven optimization to run parts of paid media, email, CRM journeys, retargeting

item.title: [General] Meta Conversations 2026: June 3 London Date, Keynote Time, Registration, and What Businesses Should Expect
item.description: Meta has confirmed that Conversations 2026, its annual business messaging conference, will take place on Wednesday, June 3, 2026, in London, with the keynote livestream starting at 2:30 p.m. BST. On the surface, that is a straightforward event announcement. In practical terms, it is much more tha

item.title: [General] Pinterest’s “Get Off Social Media” Ad Campaign : Why the Platform Wants Users to Go Offline
item.description: Pinterest has spent years trying to separate itself from the rest of the social platform pack, but its latest brand campaign makes that distinction clearer than ever. Instead of encouraging more scrolling, more posting, and more time spent in-app, Pinterest is making a very different case: the be

item.title: [General] LinkedIn Expands AI-Powered Conversational Search to All Users: What Changed and How to Show Up in Results
item.description: LinkedIn search is changing in a way that matters far beyond one product update. The platform has expanded its AI-powered conversational search experience from a limited rollout to broader member access, which means users can increasingly search the way they naturally think and speak instead of r

item.title: [Meta Ads] Meta Simplifies Ad Performance Elements: What the New Pixel AI and One-Click Conversions API Mean for Advertisers
item.description: Meta has introduced two important updates to the infrastructure behind ad performance: a new AI-assisted enhancement for Meta Pixel and a simpler, one-click path to set up Conversions API for web. On the surface, that sounds like a technical product update. In practice, it is much more significan

item.title: [General] How X Cashtags Work: New Format, Live Price Charts, and What It Means for Market Analysis
item.description: For years, X has been one of the fastest places on the internet for price-sensitive information. Traders watch it for headlines, crypto communities use it to coordinate attention, and public companies often see market narratives take shape there before they fully spread elsewhere. That has made t

item.title: [General] Google Crawler IP Ranges in 2026: New File Location, Verification Steps, Firewall Rules, and What Site Owners Need to Update
item.description: If your website relies on firewall rules, CDN allowlists, bot management, or server-side verification to identify legitimate Google crawlers, Google’s March 2026 update matters immediately. The change itself is simple: the published JSON files for Google crawler IP ranges have moved from the olde

item.title: [General] Top Brand Monitoring Tools in 2026: Free and Paid Platforms Compared by Features, Pricing, and Best Use Case
item.description: Brand monitoring has changed. A few years ago, most teams could get by with a simple mention alert and a social media dashboard. In 2026, that is no longer enough. A brand can be discussed on news sites, review platforms, Reddit threads, YouTube comments, LinkedIn posts, podcasts, forums, newslet

item.title: [Meta Ads] 50,000 new customers per month without media buyer or a creative strategist. HOW?!
item.description:
The apparel brand Pact acquires 50,000 new customers per month.
They don’t have a media buyer or a creative strategist on staff.
So… how the heck are they doing this?
In the final episode of Ad Spend, I sit down with Kaitlin Parker, Pact’s Chief Digital Officer, to discuss how they approach growt

item.title: [General] AI Video Generator: Beat Competition & Rank High #shorts
item.description: Understanding your competition is key for any product. For AI video generators, a crowded market demands strategic promotion and SEO to get on trusted lists like Zapier, Reddit, and Quora. #AIvideo #Competition #MarketingStrategy #SEOtips #VideoGeneration

item.title: [General] AI vs Human Ad Copy: Google vs Meta #shorts
item.description: AI ad copy can outperform humans, but human touch is crucial. Don't let your ads sound robotic. Especially on Meta, where copywriting truly matters. Explore the balance. #AdCopy #AICopywriting #MarketingTips #DigitalMarketing

item.title: [General] 魔法の施策は存在しない。泥臭いPDCAでCVを2倍にした広告運用の振り返り
item.description:
「成果改善の一発逆転ホームラン施策はないだろうか」「媒体仕様をハックして、競合を出し抜けないか」「今すぐCPAを下げたいんだけど、配信量を下げるしかないか…」 場合によっては、1つの設定を変えるだけで…

The post 魔法の施策は存在しない。泥臭いPDCAでCVを2倍にした広告運用の振り返り first appeared on アナグラム株式会社.

Advertisers can now import Google Ads Performance Max campaigns that use new customer acquisition goals into Mic

item.title: [General] Best AI Sales Outreach Tools in 2026: 20 Platforms Compared for Email, LinkedIn, Calling, and AI SDR Workflows
item.description: Sales outreach software has changed fast, but the core job has not. Teams still need to identify the right accounts, reach the right contacts, personalize at scale, follow up consistently, and turn activity into meetings and pipeline. What has changed is the amount of manual work that can now be

item.title: [General] Inside the U.S. Navy Healthcare Recruiting Campaign: Why VML and WPP Focused on Physicians, Nurses, and Dentists in 4 Major Markets
item.description: The U.S. Navy’s new healthcare recruiting campaign is notable not because it is loud, broad, or designed for mass appeal, but because it does the opposite. Instead of speaking to everyone, it speaks to a narrow professional audience: early-career physicians, nurses, and dentists, especially those

item.title: [General] Multimodal Enterprise Software: Why 80% of Enterprise Applications Could Be Multimodal by 2030
item.description: Enterprise software is moving beyond text boxes, forms, dashboards, and isolated chat interfaces. The next major shift is multimodal software: applications that can work across text, voice, image, video, documents, and numerical data inside the same workflow. That matters because employees and cu

item.title: [General] Target Pokémon 30th Anniversary Collection: Release Date, Best Items, Prices, and What Shoppers Should Know
item.description: For a retailer with a large national footprint, a franchise as recognizable as Pokémon offers more than a seasonal licensing opportunity. It offers a chance to meet several audiences at once: adults who grew up with the original Kanto era, families shopping for current fans, collectors looking fo

item.title: [General] Legora and Jude Law: Inside the “Law Just Got More Attractive” Campaign and What It Means for Legal AI Marketing
item.description: When Legora put Jude Law at the center of its new global campaign, the immediate headline was obvious. The actor’s surname made the creative line easy to remember, the visuals were polished enough to feel cinematic, and the execution stood out in a category that usually communicates through produ

item.title: [General] Bud Light x Posty Co. Minis: Post Malone’s Stagecoach Launch, Release Date, Sizes, and Where Fans Can Find Them
item.description: If you want the short version first, here it is: Bud Light x Posty Co. Minis are a limited-edition Bud Light release created with Post Malone to mark 10 years of partnership between the artist and the brand. The product comes in 7-ounce bottles and 7.5-ounce cans, makes its event debut around Sta

item.title: [General] Netflix Ad Revenue Is on Track to Reach $3 Billion in 2026: What Q1 Earnings Reveal for Advertisers, Streaming, and the Ad-Supported Tier
item.description: Netflix’s latest quarterly results did more than confirm another strong revenue period. They also gave the clearest view yet into how large the company’s advertising business is becoming, how quickly it is scaling, and why that matters well beyond Netflix itself. The headline is straightforward:

item.title: [General] Top AI Marketing Tools in 2026: 25 Platforms for SEO, Content, Ads, Email, Social Media, Analytics, and Automation
item.description: Artificial intelligence has moved from an optional layer in the marketing stack to a practical operating system for modern teams. item.title: [Microsoft Ads] Microsoft Ads Seasonality Adjustments for Portfolio Bid Strategies: What Changed and How to Use Them
item.description: Microsoft Advertising’s April 2026 update added a small line item that can have a meaningful impact on how sophisticated advertisers manage short-term demand spikes: seasonality adjustments now support campaigns that use portfolio bid strategies. On the surface, that sounds like a straightforward

item.title: [Meta Ads] Meta Announces No-Cost, One-Click Conversions API Solution
item.description: Meta recently announced a no-cost, one-click Conversions API solution. This could be a really big deal, especially for small businesses that have avoided CAPI due to costs and complicated setup.

item.title: [Google Ads] Multi-factor authentication requirement for the Google Ads API
item.description: As part of improving security for Google Ads accounts, the Google Ads API will start requiring Multi-factor authentication (MFA) for Google Ads API users. These important security updates will start rolling out from April 21, 2026, and will be enabled for all users over the next

On the surface, that sounds like a straightforward product update. In practice, it marks something much larger: the continued shift of digital discovery

item.title: [General] Meta Quest 3 and Quest 3S Price Increase: New Prices, Why Meta Raised Them, and What Buyers Need to Know

The move affects the Meta Quest 3 and Meta Quest 3S lineup, and it matters for

item.title: [General] Facebook Camera Roll Cloud Processing: What Meta Is Asking For, What It Can Access, and How to Turn It Off
item.description: Facebook is testing and expanding a feature that asks users to let the app analyze photos and videos in their phone’s camera roll so it can suggest content to post, build collages, create recaps, and recommend AI-assisted edits. On its face, the pitch is simple: many people take far more photos t

item.title: [General] Instagram Expands “Your Algorithm” to Explore: What the New Controls Mean for Discovery, Reach, and Content Strategy
item.description: Instagram has expanded its “Your Algorithm” feature beyond Reels and into Explore, giving users a more direct way to influence the topics they want to see more of, the topics they want to see less of, and the kinds of recommendations that follow them across key discovery surfaces in the app. That

item.title: [General] Google Immersive Map View Search Ads : What the New 3D Maps Ad Experience Means for Local PPC
item.description: Google appears to be testing or rolling out a richer local ad experience that brings immersive map view visuals directly into paid search placements. At first glance, that may sound like a small interface change. It is not. If this format expands, it could change how users evaluate local business

item.title: [Google Ads] Google Ads Adds “Messages from Your Ads” in Search: What It Means, How It Works, and How to Optimize It
item.description: For years, most Search advertisers have optimized around the same familiar actions: clicks, calls, form fills, purchases, and store visits. The introduction of “Messages from your ads” as an optimization goal in Search adds a different kind of intent signal to that list. It gives advertisers a wa

item.title: [Google Ads] Microsoft Performance Max Landing Page Reporting in 2026: How to Use Final URL Data to Improve ROAS, Conversion Rates, and Spend Efficiency
item.description: Microsoft Advertising has added landing page reporting for Performance Max, giving advertisers something they have wanted from automated campaign types for a long time: clearer visibility into which final URLs are actually driving results. For teams managing Performance Max at scale, this is not

item.title: [General] U.S. Search Ad Revenue Reached $114.2 Billion in 2025: What Slower Growth Means for Digital Advertising
item.description: Search advertising remained the single largest force in U.S. digital media in 2025, reaching $114.2 billion in revenue. That figure alone explains why search still sits at the center of most performance marketing strategies. It delivers scale, it captures demand close to the point of intent, and

item.title: [General] ChatGPT Ads : What Advertisers Know, What OpenAI Has Confirmed, and Why ROI Is Still Unclear

That shift was always going to raise the same question that follows every scaled attention platform: when does advertising arrive, and what happens when i

item.title: [General] What Is Google Discover? How It Works, What Drives Visibility, and How to Increase Discover Traffic
item.description: Google Discover is one of the most important traffic sources many publishers, brands, and content teams still underuse. It does not work like standard Google Search, and that difference matters. Search responds to a user’s query. Discover tries to predict what a user may want to read, watch, or e

item.description:

item.description: Most pages do not underperform because the writer lacked effort. They underperform because the page was built around the wrong search intent, the wrong structure, or the wrong expectations. A business may publish a long article, add the target keyword a few times, and still see little movement be

item.title: [General] AI Agents for Marketing: 15 Proven Use Cases, ROI Metrics, and a Practical Implementation Guide for 2026
item.description: Marketing teams have spent years automating pieces of the funnel. Someone had to decide what to do next,

item.title: [General] White-Label Partnerships as Scalable Infrastructure: The $99B Opportunity for Agencies, SaaS Companies, and Service Brands
item.description: White-label partnerships are often described as a sales tactic, a fulfillment shortcut, or an easy way to add services without hiring. That description is too narrow. In practice, the strongest white-label partnerships function as scalable infrastructure. They let a business add capabilities, ent

item.title: [Google Ads] Digital Marketing Trends in 2026: 10 Data-Backed Shifts Shaping SEO, AI Search, Video, and Demand Generation
item.description: Digital marketing trends are no longer just about adopting the newest platform or testing the latest tool. The bigger shift is structural. Search behavior has changed. Social platforms now function like discovery engines. Video is not just a branding format anymore. AI is influencing research, co

item.title: [General] NYCFC’s Sky Scraper : Why New York City FC Chose a Pigeon as Its Official Mascot
item.description: When New York City FC made Sky Scraper its official mascot, the decision made immediate sense to anyone who has spent time around the club, its supporters, or the city itself. For years, the pigeon had already lived in NYCFC culture. It showed up in fan identity, in informal symbolism, and in the

item.title: [General] Technical SEO Checklist for 2026: 34 Critical Fixes for Crawlability, Indexing, Core Web Vitals, and AI Search Visibility
item.description: Technical SEO is the part of search optimization that decides whether your site can be discovered, rendered, understood, and trusted before content quality or backlinks can do their job. If the technical foundation is weak, even strong pages can struggle to rank consistently. If the foundation is

item.title: [General] Earned Media: What It Is, Why It Matters, and How to Build a Strategy That Earns Trust, Links, and Brand Visibility
item.description: If people talk about your brand when you are not paying them and not publishing the message yourself, that attention falls into a category marketers call earned media. It is one of the oldest ideas in marketing, but it matters in a more practical way now because visibility no longer depends on on

item.title: [General] What Is AI Discover? A Practical Guide to AI Search Visibility, Brand Citations, and AI Overviews
item.description: People no longer discover brands only through ten blue links. They ask broader questions, compare options inside AI-generated answers, and often make shortlists before they ever visit a website. That change is what makes AI Discover an important topic for marketers, business owners, publishers, a

item.title: [General] Local Link Building for Local SEO: 15 Proven Tactics, Outreach Templates, and a 90-Day Plan
item.description: If your business depends on customers in a specific city, region, or service area, local link building is not a side tactic. It is part of how search engines decide whether your business is credible, prominent, and relevant enough to surface for local searches. That matters because local search b

In practical terms, that means a site can present a monetization layer after a reader reac

That approach was weak five years ago. In 2026, it is even weaker. Search has changed. Google now answers more broad informational queries directly. AI O

item.title: [Meta Ads] TikTok vs. Instagram Ads in Q2 2026: Cost, ROI, Audience, and the Best Budget Split

item.title: [General] Snap Cuts 16% of Staff: What the Layoffs Mean for Snapchat, AI, Advertisers, and Profitability

The message from CEO Evan Spiegel was direct. Snap wants to move faster, operate more effi

They want websites that remember useful preferences, email programs that reflect actual interests, product recommendations that make sense, and customer journeys that fee

item.title: [Google Ads] April 2026 update to Display & Video 360 API
item.description:
Today we’re announcing the April 2026 update to the Display & Video 360 API. This update includes the following:

  • Support for additional

item.title: [Microsoft Ads] Publisher release roundup: Q1 2026 edition
item.description: Check out our Q1 2026 Microsoft Monetize updates that are focused on delivering practical improvements across usability, deals, and ad quality

item.title: [Meta Ads] How to PREDICT a Winning Ad creative in 30 seconds
item.description:
Get personal Meta Ads feedback from me (live) for less than $60 per session: 👉 https://www.skool.com/benheath/about
Let my agency run your Meta ads for you: 👉 https://heathmedia.co.uk/done-for-you/
If you’re new here — I’m Ben Heath.
I run Heath Media, a marketing agency that helps businesses sca

item.title: [Google Ads] PMAX, AI MAX & The Future of Google Ads
item.description: In this interview I am joined by 2 of my Senior Google Ads Growth Strategists from Define Digital Academy, Brent Neale and Johan Carlberger, as we break down what's working with Performance Max, the potential of AI Max, keyword cannibalization, bidding strategies and how AI is reshaping Google Ads.

item.title: [Google Ads] Google Ads Copy: Fast AI Method for Clients #shorts
item.description: Struggling with Google Ads copy? Use your client's landing page content to generate Performance Max ad copy instantly. A streamlined process for physical therapy practices. #GoogleAds #AdCopy #DigitalMarketing #PhysicalTherapy

item.title: [Meta Ads] Do You Need to Warm Up a New Ad Account?
item.description: Today's question is about whether a brand new ad account, Facebook page, and pixel need any special treatment before running sales campaigns. Conventional wisdom says to build followers first, run awareness campaigns, or season the pixel with top-of-funnel activity. Jon explains why these recommenda

item.title: [Google Ads] How to Create GA4 Audiences and Use Them in Google Ads
item.description: If you're running Google Ads, you can use audiences from Google Analytics to target your campaigns and reach the right people. In this tutorial, I'll walk you through how to create an audience in GA4, check that consent is configured correctly, and apply your audience in Google Ads. To do this, we'l

item.title: [Google Ads] We’re upgrading Dynamic Search Ads to AI Max
item.description: AI Max is moving out of beta, with improved performance quality across targeting and creative capabilities along with more enhanced controls.

item.title: [LINE Ads] 【2026年4月統合開始】LINEヤフー広告 ディスプレイ広告への移行ガイド。運用者がチェックすべきポイントを解説
item.description:
2026年4月に、従来の LINE 広告と Yahoo! 広告が統合され「LINE ヤフー広告 ディスプレイ広告」の提供が開始されました。 これに伴い、運用担当者やマーケターの方は「結局、自分は何か作業をしなければならな …

"【2026年4月統合開始】LINEヤフー広告 ディスプレイ広告への移行ガイド。運用者がチェック

item.title: [General] Scaling Paid Media Without Burning Cash: Smarter Testing Frameworks for Modern PPC
item.description: Scaling paid media looks simple, but what usually happens is this: you push spend, performance holds for a few days, then CPAs start creeping up.  Branded and high-intent campaigns carry the account for a while, but the moment you lean into broader queries or new audiences, efficiency drops.

item.title: [General] From Clicks to Confidence: How Brands Validate PPC Performance Without Flawed Attribution
item.description: Thanks to AI and platform tools, PPC campaigns can be created fairly quickly.  That’s the easy part. But how do you measure results and attribute clicks to the right campaign?   Cross-device journeys made things messy, then Privacy changes made things even worse. Platforms fill in

item.title: [Google Ads] Why Your Google Ads Aren’t Getting Clicks
item.description: If you’ve started running Google Ads and no one is clicking, it can feel frustrating. You might wonder if you’re doing something wrong or if ads just don’t work. The truth is, Google Ads do work, but small mistakes can stop them from performing. The good news is that most problems are easy to fix

item.title: [Google Ads] Google Keyword Planner: How to Find High-Intent Keywords, Estimate Search Demand, and Build Smarter SEO & PPC Campaigns
item.description: Google Keyword Planner has been around for years, but it still holds a unique place in keyword research because the data comes from Google Ads. That matters. When you want to understand how Google groups search demand, how advertisers value a query, and how keyword interest changes by location or

item.title: [General] Digital Agency Client Onboarding Checklist: 27 Best Practices, Questions, and Templates
item.description: Winning a new client is expensive. Keeping that client long enough to produce meaningful results is where agency economics start to work. That is why client onboarding matters more than most agencies admit. It is not administrative cleanup after the sale. It is the period when expectations are se

item.title: [General] 16 Best Websites for Digital Marketing Jobs in 2026: Where to Find Remote, Freelance, and Full-Time Roles
item.description: If you are looking for digital marketing jobs, the biggest mistake is treating every job site as if it does the same thing. It does not. Some platforms are best for volume. Some are best for remote roles. Some are best for startup jobs. Some are built for freelancers who want projects instead of

item.title: [General] Top SEO Plugins for WordPress Compared: Yoast vs Rank Math vs AIOSEO vs SEOPress vs The SEO Framework vs Slim SEO
item.description: If you search for the best SEO plugin for WordPress, you will usually see the same names appear again and again: Yoast SEO, Rank Math, All in One SEO, SEOPress, The SEO Framework, and Slim SEO. That is not an accident. These are the plugins most site owners actually compare when they want one too

item.title: [General] Top Digital Marketing Courses in 2026: 10 Online Programs Worth Your Time for SEO, PPC, Social Media, Email and Analytics
item.description: If you search for the top digital marketing courses, you run into a familiar problem fast: there are too many options, most of them promise the same outcome, and very few explain who each course is actually for. That matters because “digital marketing” is not one skill. It is a stack. Search, pai

item.title: [General] Meta Conversations 2026 Date Announced: What the June 3 London Event Means for WhatsApp, Messenger, and Instagram Business Messaging
item.description: Meta has confirmed that Conversations 2026, its annual business messaging conference, will take place on June 3 in London, with a global livestream available for registered viewers. On the surface, that sounds like a straightforward event notice. In practice, it is more than that. Conversations h

item.title: [General] Bud Light NFL Draft Keg Wall 2026: How Free Draft Beer Works in Pittsburgh and 9 Partner Markets
item.description: Bud Light is tying one of the NFL calendar’s most watched offseason moments to a simple, easy-to-understand fan offer: when your team is on the clock, a draft beer is on Bud Light. That idea sits at the center of the brand’s 2026 NFL Draft activation in Pittsburgh, where Bud Light is introducing
